criteria.pass_condition: The Swift PID owns no local-terminal PTY master and allocates no canonical Ghostty terminal.

The Swift PID performs no terminal shaping, terminal render encoding, or terminal draws and submits at most one labeled full-surface blit per admitted visible frame; renderer PIDs own terminal GPU work.
Every accepted frame records worker audit identity, renderer epoch, presentation generation, and frame sequence.
Force-killing and relaunching Swift preserves shell PID, TTY, cwd, terminal ID, topology, scrollback sentinel, and unread activity.
Killing one renderer during output preserves the shell and unrelated workspaces, creates a new worker epoch, installs a full scene, and never presents a stale frame.
Moving a terminal during numbered output preserves PID and terminal ID, displays every number once, and never presents the retired generation.
Concurrent GUI and TUI viewers at different sizes do not oscillate the PTY; only the geometry lease holder changes canonical dimensions.
Concurrent GUI, TUI, and automation input follows the specified total order without split paste, lost release, or duplicate command.
Each visible topology mutation is one daemon transaction and revision, and client projections converge after reconnect and overflow.
Swift and browser renderer paths receive no raw PTY output and instantiate no second canonical parser.
Embedded and external rendering match for ASCII, ligatures, emoji, CJK, combining text, wide cells, styles, cursor, palette, and OSC colors within the documented tolerance.
Links, selection, search, Kitty images, custom shaders, synchronized output, and IME match the embedded terminal behavior.
Sustained active and background output causes no visible sidebar hitch and stays within the approved interaction-latency budget.
One hundred dormant workspaces create no renderer workers; worker count follows visible terminal workspaces.
Slow or stopped clients cannot block PTY parsing, topology mutations, or other clients; overflow forces a full resnapshot with bounded retained bytes.
Unauthenticated, stale-capability, malformed, and oversized requests are rejected before state mutation or allocation amplification.
Compatible protocol ranges reconnect; incompatible ranges fail read-only with an actionable error and unchanged state.
VoiceOver text, cursor, selection, links, and focus match canonical terminal state while rendering is remote.
Each supported launch mode has one terminal authority, and backend mode has no local fallback capable of owning the same terminal.
